zone_6b_notes: Start indoors 8â€“10 weeks before last frost (mid-February in Farmington) â€” eggplant needs a long head start. Transplant after last frost when soil is reliably warm (late May). Eggplant is the most heat-demanding vegetable in the garden; Missouri summers suit it well once established. Black Beauty (globe) and Ichiban (Japanese/slender) both perform well. Harvest while skin is glossy â€” dull skin means over-mature and seeds are hard. Flea beetles are the primary pest in Missouri; row cover until flowering helps significantly.

planting.best_planting_time: Transplant late May when soil is warm

water: consistent â€” 1â€“2 inches/week; drought causes bitter fruit
